{{Quote|We have obvious parodies with Luke and Darth Vader, but it's the bizarre little ones that fans will really have to know their trivia. We did our research. After all, we're fans making a special for fans.|Corey Campodonico, co-producer of the special|<ref name="Producers">{{SWArchive|url=community/news/media/f20070612/index.html|text="Robot Chicken" Producers Talk ''Star Wars''|date=2007-06-12}}</ref>}}
'''''Robot Chicken: Star Wars''''' is a ''[[Star Wars]]''-themed double-length special of the [[Wikipedia:Sketch comedy|sketch comedy]] series [[Wikipedia:Robot Chicken|''Robot Chicken'']] that first aired on [[Cartoon Network]]'s [[Wikipedia:Adult Swim|Adult Swim]] block on [[June 17]], [[2007]]. It features the voices of [[George Lucas]], [[Mark Hamill]], [[Ahmed Best]], [[Tom Kane]], [[Wikipedia:Joey Fatone|Joey Fatone]] and [[Bob Bergen]]. It proved to be highly popular and spawned a [[Robot Chicken: Star Wars Episode II|sequel]], which aired on [[November 16]], [[2008]].

In addition to new material, the special also features several ''Star Wars''-themed sketches from previous episodes.

==Sketches {{SectionFlag}}==
Between each sketch section, static flares across the screen to signify the change.
*The title sequence features a thematically altered version of the show's standard opening and the reconstruction scene of [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] following his battle on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]]. A mad [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] recovers a dead fowl by a stream of lava, revives the bird by using [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] and a dissection saw, and then binds "Darth Chicken" to a chair and forces him to watch a wall of [[television set]]s featuring clips from the special.
*During the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] destroys an [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] by grappling onto it, and throws a [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]] into a bathroom occupied by an unsuspecting [[Vehicle Command Crewer|Imperial Army pilot]] reading a copy of ''Naboobies'' magazine.
*While [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine is describing his [[Skirmish in the Senate|duel]] with [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] in the [[Grand Convocation Chamber]] and laughing about it with [[Mas Amedda/Legends|Mas Amedda]] and [[Sim Aloo]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] calls to inform him that the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] has just been destroyed, throwing Palpatine into a fit about the financial loss incurred with this disaster. (This sketch was reused from the episode ''1987''.)
*The bartender in [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina]] asks for a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa's]] order, to which the Jawa shouts "Martini!"
*A janitor sweeps up the halves of [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] and decides to be transferred to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].
*[[File:AckbarCereal.jpg|thumb|right|100px|Admiral Ackbar Cereal.]]Admiral [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] stars in a commercial for a cereal bearing his name.
*Industrial designer [[Ponda Baba/Legends|Ponda Baba's]] conflict with [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in the Mos Eisley Cantina is shown to be the result of a failure to communicate caused by his compulsive joker friend [[Cornelius Evazan/Legends|Cornelius Evazan]], who has become too drunk to translate Ponda's words. (Also featured in ''Adultizzle Swizzle'', an alternate version of the episode ''The Sack''.)
*[[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] encounter problems at an airport screening line thanks to their metallic structure.
*[[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] attempts to pass his ignited lightsaber to Obi-Wan, but Obi-Wan drops it with peace-disturbing consequences.
*An Imperial officer witnesses a slight (and embarrassing) technical difficulty in [[Darth Vader's meditation chamber/Legends|Darth Vader's meditation chamber]].
*New Imperial Death Star officer recruits receive orientation about Darth Vader's [[Telekinesis/Legends#Choking powers|Force choke]] being merely a delusion and the right behavior to avoid a more permanent death by his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].
*After destroying the Death Star I, Luke excitedly intends to call his [[Owen Lars/Legends|uncle]] and [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|aunt]] about his achievement, only to remember, mid-request, that they were killed.
*[[File:Boba_Fett_Fan.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Obsessive fans see George Lucas.]] At a Star Wars convention, [[George Lucas]] (voiced by himself) meets the Nerd (a regular character from ''Robot Chicken'') dressed as a [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]] and ends up being saved by that very nerd from an entire horde of nerds gone wild, bestowing a piece of fond memory to his rescuer.
*Ben Kenobi exploits Luke's temporary helplessness during their training session on the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] to give air to his more sadistic side by kneeing Luke in the crotch.
*After a British-accented [[Exogorth/Legends|space slug]] loses the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], he and his companion decide to order a humongous order of Chinese take-out.
*The janitor ends up sweeping up the corpse of [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] after it falls from Palpatine's office on Coruscant and decides to be transferred to the Death Star.
*Then-President [[Wikipedia:George W. Bush|George W. Bush]] learns that he has Jedi powers and decides to use them to have a little bit of fun—which includes a lightsaber duel with [[Wikipedia:Abraham Lincoln|Abraham Lincoln]] at the [[Wikipedia:Lincoln Memorial|Lincoln Memorial]]—only to find out that it was just all a fanciful dream after he, in a similar manner to ''Empire Strikes Back'', accidentally killed his daughter in a rage by slicing off her middle finger after she flipped him off when citing her actual dad would let her "go clubbing all she wants". (Recycled from the episode ''Massage Chair''.)
*The [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] 4 Weather Tracker predicts more cloudy weather for [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]].
*A vagrant has already taken up residence in the tauntaun that [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] slices open in an attempt to save Luke.
*During their confrontation on the [[Death Star II/Legends|Death Star II]] Luke and the Emperor engage in a "Yo Momma" fight with Darth Vader as the judge. The Emperor loses and Vader throws him into the shaft.
*The janitor ends up sweeping up Palpatine's corpse which has just landed right before his feet. (This is a reference to the opening sketch of [[Wikipedia:Amazon Women on the Moon|''Amazon Women on the Moon'']].)
*Han's radio conversation about reactors in the detention level becomes even more complicated when it necessitates a call to Vader's office and an examination of the schematics.
*Darth Vader is paid a visit by [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]]. Vader, with less patience for Jar Jar than he used to have, throws him out of an airlock, only for Jar Jar to return later as an annoying [[Force ghost/Legends|Force ghost]].
*The true intention of Luke's excuse to his uncle Owen about going to "[[Tosche Station/Legends|Toshi Station]] to pick up some [[Power converter/Legends|power converters]]" is revealed.
*[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] celebrates the capture of Han aboard [[Slave I/Legends|''Slave I'']] first by bragging and then by having an intimate moment with his frozen prisoner.
*[[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] re-enacts Fonz's hair-combing gag from the intro to [[Wikipedia:Happy Days|''Happy Days'']].
*Darth Vader tries to win Luke over to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] in Cloud City by revealing some secrets from the ''Star Wars'' movies. These include that he is Luke's father, Leia is his sister, the Empire will be destroyed by [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]], that he had built C-3PO, and finally that the Force is anything but mystical. Luke, fed up with his unprofessional behavior, leaves. (Recycled from the episode ''Vegetable Funfest''.)
*The Emperor's taunting of Luke is interrupted by noisy ongoing construction work on the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]].
*[[Lobot/Legends|Lobot's]] [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetic implants]] are actually headphones, and he dances to "[[The Force Theme|Force Theme]]" in Cloud City's halls.
*A commercial for [[Max Rebo/Legends|Max Rebo's]] new album containing his greatest hits, all of which reiterate that he is absolutely ''not'' an elephant.
*An episode of ''Mid-Nite with [[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckuss]]'' shows that the bigwigs of the Galactic Empire have less patience with loose-mouthed talk show hosts than one could hope for.
*Luke and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] are seen in a very intimate—and [[Wikipedia:Incest|very inappropriate]]—moment together.
*An ice show version of ''Star Wars Episode V: The Empire Strikes Back'', called ''Empire on Ice'' (which reiterates the events on [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]), makes a performance.
*End credits: chickens "bawk" the ''Star Wars'' credits music.
*After the end credits, there is an extra scene with Jar Jar Binks continuing to annoy Darth Vader while he futilely tries to sleep in his ''Star Wars'' merchandise-filled room.
*The "Stoopid Monkey" at the end has cut off his hand with a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]].

===Notes===
In the segment with the [[Wikipedia:Vietnam Veterans Memorial|Vietnam Veterans Memorial]], the creators inserted their names in the place of some of the veterans, including Seth Green (quite clearly) and Matthew Senreich (partially obstructed). Senreich is also seen in a portrait hanging over the fireplace in the Oval Office, replacing the portrait of George Washington.

In the segment where a ghostly Jar Jar Binks haunts Darth Vader while he sleeps, four [[Star Wars Miniatures]] (a stormtrooper commander, a scout trooper, a stormtrooper and an R2-D2) can be seen on Vader's nightstand. Vader is also sleeping under ''Star Wars'' sheets, and the bed frame resembles his meditation chamber.

During the sketch featuring then-president Bush and former president Clinton, the restaurant is denoted by a single yellow arch on a red bar. This is an obvious reference to the double arches of [[Wikipedia:McDonald's|McDonald's]]. As well, former president Clinton wanted a Big Mac, a main dish of the restaurant.

The Power Converters seem to be the dancers in the [[Max Rebo Band/Legends|Max Rebo Band]].

In the sketch with Ponda Baba, a few objects are what seems to be [[IG-88B|IG-88's]] head: the water cooler in Ponda's office and some machines in the cantina seen behind the counter. These are in reference to the pieces used in ''A New Hope'' from which IG-88's head was later made. Also, in Ponda's bedroom there is a picture of Ponda on his left wall. Ponda's address is [[1138 (easter egg)|1138]].

During the sketch with ''Mid-Nite with [[Zuckuss/Legends|Zuckuss]]'', a mug in the shape of [[Boushh/Legends|Boushh's]] helmet is visible on the desk as a reference to [[Wikipedia:Conan O'Brien|Conan O'Brien's]] Eisenhower mug.

There is no skit based on [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], although [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] is mentioned.

The show contains at least half a dozen censored uses of the word "fuck," most of them from the second sketch.

The second sketch, "Vader Calls the Emperor," was featured in a regular episode of ''Robot Chicken'', in which Darth Vader had an uncharacteristically deep voice. This voice has been redone in the ''Star Wars'' special so it sounds more like [[James Earl Jones]].

In the space slugs sketch, the ''Millennium Falcon''{{'s}} cockpit is on the left side, instead of right.

==Release and reception==
Over its four airings, the special received 5.7 million viewers ages 12 and up. It helped Adult Swim regain the status as the #1 cable network for total day delivery of young adults ages 18–34 and 18–24.<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.animationinsider.net/article.php?articleID=1395|text='Robot Chicken: Star Wars' Ratings|author=Bynum, Aaron H.|date=2007-06-20|work=Animation Insider|archivedate=20121023203056}}</ref>

On [[July 22]], 2008, Warner Home Video released ''Robot Chicken: Star Wars'' on DVD. The single-disc release includes the special itself plus many special features:<ref>{{WebCite|url=http://www.tvshowsondvd.com/news/Robot-Chicken-Star-Wars/8772|text=Robot Chicken - 'Not Long Ago, In a Galaxy Not Far Enough Away': ''Robot Chicken - Star Wars'' Comes to DVD!|author=Lambert, David|date=2008-01-11|work=tvshowsondvd.com|archivedate=20160730001615}}</ref><ref name="SWArchive-Robot Chicken: Star Wars on DVD">{{SWArchive|url=community/news/media/news20080326.html|text=Robot Chicken: ''Star Wars'' on DVD}}</ref>

*Seven different audio commentaries, including actors, writers, production crew and [[Jett Lucas|Jett]] and [[Katie Lucas]].
*"Chicken Nuggets" sketch by sketch video commentary - previously available on adultswim.com
*Animation Meeting
*On-Air Bumps
*Trailers
*Behind-the-Scenes Featurette
*Deleted Scenes
*Alternate Audio
*Time Lapse Sequences
*Photo Gallery
*STAR WARS Celebration IV Convention Panel Presentation
*Production Design Featurette
*a hidden Easter Egg in the language selection which features a short film about the crew's trouble in composing proper musical scores for the ''Empire On Ice'' sketch

This special was alluded to at the end of the [[Wikipedia:Family Guy|''Family Guy'']] ''Star Wars'' special "[[Wikipedia:Blue Harvest|Blue Harvest]]" as an in-joke as Seth Green, one of the creators of ''Robot Chicken'', also plays [[Wikipedia:Chris Griffin|Chris Griffin]] on ''Family Guy''. Chris breaks the fourth wall by saying, "But didn't ''Robot Chicken'' do this a few months ago?" His father, [[Wikipedia:Peter Griffin|Peter]], then starts saying how bad ''Robot Chicken'' is and that no one knows about the show. Angered by this, Chris runs from the room, although not before angrily calling out Peter as being "a really big jerk". The sequel specials [[Wikipedia:Something, Something, Something, Dark Side|''Something, Something, Something, Dark Side'']] and to a lesser extent [[Wikipedia:It's a Trap!|''Family Guy: It's A Trap!'']] featured similar references to ''Robot Chicken'', the former where Peter tries to dare ''Robot Chicken'' to "top" his story only for Chris to state it already did and was determined not to let Peter get to him, but ultimately stormed out of the room when Peter off-handedly mentioned that Chris could tell ''Without a Paddle'' (an allusion to Seth Green's role in the failed film [[Wikipedia:Without a Paddle|of the same name]]).
